sync_rules.name: programbuild_control_inventory
sync_rules.description: Changes to Program Build control behavior should update the canonical and file index docs in the same change.

sync_rules.name: programbuild_architecture_contracts
sync_rules.description: Test strategy, release readiness, and risk spikes depend on architecture contracts. Update ARCHITECTURE.md first when contracts change.

sync_rules.name: programbuild_requirements_scope
sync_rules.description: User flows, architecture, and test strategy must stay aligned with requirements scope. Update REQUIREMENTS.md first when scope changes.

sync_rules.name: programbuild_feasibility_cascade
sync_rules.description: Feasibility outcomes, kill criteria, and early risk framing should be updated before downstream research, scope, and spike plans drift.

sync_rules.name: programbuild_variant_alignment
sync_rules.description: Variant playbooks should stay compatible with the master playbook stage order and naming rules. Review PROGRAMBUILD.md when variants change.

sync_rules.name: userjourney_external_review_packet
sync_rules.description: External legal and operational review framing must stay aligned with the open questions, legal notes, and consent authority docs.

sync_rules.name: userjourney_route_state_logic
sync_rules.description: Route or state behavior changes must update the decision and route/state authority docs before dependent execution docs.

sync_rules.name: userjourney_legal_consent_behavior
sync_rules.description: Legal or consent behavior changes must update the authority docs before drafts, copy, and acceptance criteria.

sync_rules.name: userjourney_ux_surfaces_copy
sync_rules.description: UX surface changes should update inventory, flows, and copy before downstream acceptance and analytics docs.

sync_rules.name: userjourney_implementation_sequence
sync_rules.description: Implementation sequence changes must start in the tracker and then flow to slices, file checklist, and delivery gameplan.

sync_rules.name: decisions_adr_template
sync_rules.description: ADR format is defined in PROGRAMBUILD_ADR_TEMPLATE.md. New decision records in docs/decisions/ must follow that template. If the template format changes, review existing records for alignment.

sync_rules.name: commit_enforcement_alignment
sync_rules.description: The Conventional Commits instruction file is the authority for commit message rules. Changes to the enforcement script or gitlint spec must be consistent with it. The pre-commit config is intentionally excluded because drift operates at file granularity and unrelated hook edits must not require a commit-policy authority touch.

sync_rules.name: knowledge_base_docs_alignment
sync_rules.description: README.md knowledge-base workflow guidance must stay aligned with the KB data model and the canonical knowledge-base operations docs. README changes about KB refresh behavior require a paired authority-file change.

sync_rules.name: automation_gate_jit_alignment
sync_rules.description: Nox sessions, editor tasks, and the JIT source-of-truth protocol instruction file must stay aligned. When any of the three authority files change, review the others for alignment before updating copilot-instructions.md or QUICKSTART.md.

sync_rules.name: architecture_decision_alignment
sync_rules.description: When ARCHITECTURE.md contracts change, DECISION_LOG.md must carry the corresponding decision update in the same change. Decisions can still be added independently.

sync_rules.name: requirements_test_alignment
sync_rules.description: Test strategy must stay aligned with requirements. When requirements change, review TEST_STRATEGY.md for coverage gaps. When TEST_STRATEGY.md changes, confirm the change is driven by a requirements update.

sync_rules.name: pyproject_requirements_sync
sync_rules.description: requirements.txt is auto-generated from pyproject.toml via 'uv export --extra dev'. pyproject.toml is the authority for dependency declarations. When pyproject.toml changes, regenerate requirements.txt with: uv export --format requirements-txt --extra dev --no-hashes -o requirements.txt

sync_rules.name: process_registry_schema_enforcement
sync_rules.description: The process registry schema and composed registry manifest are the authority for registry structure. Schema enforcement code, tests, and hook wiring must stay aligned with them.
